name: BETA Technologies, Inc.
business: BETA Technologies designs, manufactures, and sells high-performance electric aircraft, advanced electric propulsion systems, charging systems, and components, with a focus on the enabling technologies of batteries, motors, flight control systems, and charging infrastructure. It targets the cargo and logistics, medical, defense, and passenger end markets.
namedCustomers: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services

exposures.sentence: We design, manufacture and sell high-performance electric aircraft, advanced electric propulsion systems, charging systems and components.
Our focus is on the Enabling Technologies essential to electric aviation, including batteries, motors, flight control systems and an international network of electric charging and related equipment.
We have also completed successful cargo and logistics demonstrations with medical partners and customers such as UPS and United Therapeutics Corporation.

Our go-to-market strategy is also incremental over time. We intend to prioritize cargo and logistics, while also giving focus to military applications and medical industries, before delivering aircraft to passenger operators.

We are redefining the aerospace industry. We have developed an electric aircraft platform and propulsion systems that are positioned to transform the aviation industry forward into a new phase of growth.
Every BETA propulsion system is designed, validated, conformed and customized to the operational requirements of our aircraft, customers and their respective use cases.

Our digital platform also drives regulatory compliance through automated maintenance record synchronization and offers seamless integration with existing aviation systems through universal API access.

catalysts.event: FAA Type Certification of H500A motor under Part 33 targeted
catalysts.sentence: We are targeting Type Certification by the FAA for the H500A under Part 33 in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: ALIA CTOL Type Certification targeted approximately 12 months after H500A certification
catalysts.sentence: We are targeting FAA Part 23 certification approximately 12 months following the Type Certification of our H500A electric motor.

catalysts.event: ALIA VTOL Type Certification targeted approximately 12 months after CTOL certification
catalysts.sentence: Our Backlog for the ALIA VTOL consists of 560 aircraft, of which 158 are Firm Orders and 402 are Options, with a goal of achieving Type Certification approximately 12 months following the Type Certification of our ALIA CTOL aircraft.

catalysts.event: BETA Operate Control Center module initial commercially-viable version targeted
catalysts.sentence: Development of the integrated Control Center module is underway, with limited functionality already in use, and an initial commercially-viable version targeted to be operational by the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: BETA Operate Network and Data modules availability expected
catalysts.sentence: The Network and Data modules remain in planning, with availability expected prior to aircraft certification, currently targeted for late 2026.

catalysts.event: EASA validation of H500A expected to start immediately following FAA Type Certification
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate we will start the validation process on H500A with EASA immediately following FAA Type Certification or in 2026.

tamClaims.claim: TAM for electric/hybrid aircraft of approximately 60,000 units valued at $250 billion through 2035
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate the TAM for electric/hybrid aircraft to consist of approximately 60,000 units with an assumed value of $250 billion, using an average selling price of approximately $4 million per unit, through 2035.

tamClaims.claim: Incremental aftermarket revenue opportunity of approximately three times initial aircraft sale, for an approximate value of $750 billion
tamClaims.sentence: In addition to initial aircraft sales, we believe that each aircraft represents an estimated three times in incremental aftermarket revenue opportunity throughout its life, for an approximate value of $750 billion.

tamClaims.claim: Demand forecast of nearly 2,000 BETA aircraft for defense applications through 2035
tamClaims.sentence: Our demand forecast consists of nearly 2,000 BETA aircraft for defense applications through 2035, based on U.S. Military estimates and internal opportunity sizing.

tamClaims.claim: Civil aircraft backlog of 891 aircraft worth $3.47 billion
tamClaims.sentence: We have an existing civil aircraft Backlog of 891 aircraft worth $3.47 billion, of which 289 are Firm Orders and 602 are Options.

tamClaims.claim: Potential 10-year opportunity up to $1 billion to supply electric pusher motors to EVE
tamClaims.sentence: The agreement represents a potential 10-year opportunity for BETA up to $1 billion subsequent to an initial evaluation period in which EVE will purchase, test and validate the performance of BETA's motors in EVE's prototype aircraft.
